title: referrals — subprocessors
description: The Referrals module itself engages ZERO third-party subprocessors — every state transition happens within the Academorix platform. Downstream marketing module engages ad-network subprocessors (Meta / Google / TikTok / Microsoft / Snapchat) to receive the marketing events fired from this module; the notifications module engages email/SMS subprocessors to deliver invitations. All are declared in their respective modules' subprocessors.json.

downstream_subprocessors_referenced.used_for: Forwarding Referral Signup Attributed + Referral Vested Attributed + reversal events to ad networks (Meta CAPI, Google Ads Enhanced Conversions, TikTok Events API, Microsoft UET, Snapchat CAPI).
Delivering ReferralInvitationSent invitations via email / SMS + ReferralClaimed / ReferralSignedUp / ReferralVested / ReferralRewarded / ReferralClawback / ReferralFraudFlagged notifications.
Processing the actual credit issuance (materialization) + refund/chargeback cascades. Stripe / Paddle / Adyen depending on region.
None — attribution module also engages zero direct subprocessors. Referrals reads AttributionSnapshotBuilder output at claim time; the snapshot itself is stored locally.

notes: This file exists deliberately with an empty subprocessors array. The GDPR Art. 30 record of processing activities requires per-module subprocessor lists; an empty list is the correct declaration for a module that engages none directly.

The Wave 5 tenant-facing 'subprocessor disclosure' page reads THIS FILE'S downstream_subprocessors_referenced list to compose the full disclosure — every downstream that this module transitively engages is surfaced to the tenant admin.

Ad-network subprocessors (Meta / Google / TikTok / Microsoft / Snapchat) are the highest-risk category — they receive PII (hashed) + click IDs + conversion values. Tenants opt into which providers via marketing module's provider configuration.
